images.caption:
Figure 1:Overview of Humanoid-GPT.
The system consists of three stages: (a) data curation and processing,
(b) training PPO-based motion experts on clusters with keypoint-level rewards,
and (c) distilling all experts into a single Transformer-based generalist policy via parallel DAgger supervision. The resulting Humanoid-GPT can take unseen or online retargeted motions as reference inputs and track them in a fully zero-shot manner.

Figure 2:Comparison of dataset diversity in the HME embedding space.Each bubble represents a dataset, where the horizontal and vertical axes denotegstdandlog-volumerespectively, and the bubble size reflects the relative amount of motion clips. Upper-right bubbles indicate broader coverage and higher diversity.

Figure 3:Real-world experiments for our Humanoid-GPT.All motions illustrated are excluded from training to verify generalization capability. Our method can track diverse, complex and high-dynamic motion in a zero-shot manner.
Figure 4:Comparison of inference latencyamong different optimization methods. Our final optimization reaches about 5 times faster than TWIST.

Figure 5:Additional Real-world experiments for our Humanoid-GPT.All motions illustrated are excluded from training to verify generalization capability. Our method can track diverse, complex and high-dynamic motion in a zero-shot manner, especially various dance motions.
Figure 6:Data Scaling up Curve on Zero-shot Performance.
Figure 10:T-SNE distribution Visualization for our dataset.
